Web Lurker Deadfall.name: Web Lurker Deadfall
Web Lurker Deadfall.description: クモの罠のトリップワイヤーが岩塊の束を解き放ち、10-フィートの範囲内にいるすべての対象に落下する.
Web Lurker Deadfall.disable: Survival (trained) or Thievery (expert) DC 20 to rearrange the webbing.

Deadfall.name: Deadfall
Deadfall.description:
Trigger A creature steps into the square with the web tripwire.

Effect All creatures in the trap's 10-foot square take @Damage[2d6[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic] save).

Sportlebore.name: Sportlebore
Sportlebore.description: A sportlebore hides amid the victuals on a plate of food or within a pack of trail rations.
Sportlebore.disable: A creature that notices the sportlebore can remove it from the food it is hidden within automatically as an Interact action.

Mimic Food.name: ミミックの食べ物/Mimic Food
Mimic Food.description:
Trigger A creature eats food the sportlebore is hidden within

Effect The sportlebore rapidly multiplies in the host's stomach, exposing the host to sportlebore infestation

Sportlebore Infestation.name: Sportlebore Infestation
Sportlebore Infestation.description:
Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:22|basic]

Stage 1 carrier with no ill effect (1 day)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} (1 hour)

Stage 3 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2} (1 hour)

Stage 4 @Damage[4d6[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:25|basic] save) as the host painfully vomits out a sportlebore swarm and returns to stage 1

Fireball Rune.name: Fireball Rune
Fireball Rune.description: An invisible rune creates an invisible, spherical magical sensor with a 20-foot radius.
Fireball Rune.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22|name:Erase the Rune] (expert) to erase the rune without triggering the sensor, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG]{Dispel Magic} (3rd rank; counteract DC 20) to counteract the rune

Fireball.name: Fireball
Fireball.description:
Trigger A living creature enters the sensor area

Effect The rune detonates a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.sxQZ6yqTn0czJxVd]{Fireball} centered on the triggering creature's square. This is a 3rd-rank fireball spell that deals @Damage[6d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic] save).

Electric Latch Rune.name: Electric Latch Rune
Electric Latch Rune.description: An invisible rune imprinted on a door latch releases a powerful electric discharge.
Electric Latch Rune.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20|name:Scratch Out the Rune|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to scratch out the rune without allowing electricity to flow, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG]{Dispel Magic} (2nd rank; counteract DC 18) to counteract the rune

Electrocution.name: Electrocution
Electrocution.description:
Trigger A creature grasps the door latch directly or with a tool

Effect The trap deals @Damage[3d12[electricity]] damage to the triggering creature (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic] save).

Malevolent Mannequins.name: Malevolent Mannequins
Malevolent Mannequins.description: A host of violent spirits possess three clockwork mannequins, and each lashes out at any creatures that dare walk near.
Malevolent Mannequins.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:37|name:Jam a mannequin's clockwork mechanisms|traits:clockwork,haunt,mechanical,hazard]{Thievery (master)} to jam a mannequin's clockwork mechanisms or a @Check[religion|dc:35|name:Exorcise a spirit from a mannequin|traits:clockwork,haunt,mechanical,hazard]{Religion (expert)} twice to exorcise a spirit from a mannequin.
Malevolent Mannequins.reset: The haunt deactivates after it has no target creatures and resets, usually over the course of 1 round, as the mannequins move to their original positions. If a mannequin is more than 150 feet from its original position, the haunt takes longer to reset, as the mannequin moves back 150 feet each round.
Malevolent Mannequins.routine: (9 actions) Each of the mannequins uses three of the haunt's actions on each turn, and the haunt loses three actions each turn for every disabled or destroyed mannequin. Each mannequin uses its first action to Stride up to 50 feet and its remaining two actions to make clockwork fist Strikes. Each has its own separate multiple attack penalty.

Awaken.name: Awaken
Awaken.description:
Trigger A creature comes within 30 feet of a mannequin

Effect The mannequins all lurch towards the triggering creature, Striding up to 50 feet, then roll initiative.

Slamming Door.name: Slamming Door
Slamming Door.description: Pressure-sensitive panels in the floor connect to a stone slab hidden in a hallway's ceiling.
Slamming Door.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:15|name:Disable Floor Panel] (trained) on the floor panels before the slab falls

Slam Shut.name: バタンと閉まる/Slam Shut
Slam Shut.description:
Trigger Pressure is placed on any floor tile.

Effect The door falls, closing off the hallway. The stone slab deals @Damage[3d8[bludgeoning]] damage to anyone beneath or adjacent to the slab when it drops and pushes them out of its space in a random direction. A creature that succeeds at a @Check[reflex|dc:17|traits:damaging-effect] save takes no damage and rolls out of the way in a random direction. On a critical success, they can choose the direction.

Lifting the fallen slab requires a successful @Check[athletics|dc:25|name:Lift a Slab Athletics|showDC:all] check.

Hitting the floor panels triggers the trap. The slab uses the same AC and saves as the trap, but it has Hardness 12, HP 48 (BT 24).

Titanic Flytrap.name: Titanic Flytrap
Titanic Flytrap.description: On the surface, a titanic flytrap appears to be a patch of the more common flytrap plant, but beneath murky waters it hides a far larger set of jaws, reaching 10 feet across, reinforced with woody branches and lined with paralytic hairs.
Titanic Flytrap.disable: @Check[survival|dc:22|name:Mislead the Flytrap's Senses] (訓練済み) ハエトリグサの重量と圧力感覚を誤認させるため
Titanic Flytrap.reset: 1 hour (or longer, after a large meal)

Snap Shut.name: パチンと閉まる/Snap Shut
Snap Shut.description:
Trigger A Small or Medium creature moves into a square that is within reach of the flytrap's hidden jaws

Effect The flytrap's jaws snap shut, making a jaws Strike against the triggering creature.

Devour.name: Devour
Devour.description: The target is trapped by the flytrap's jaws, gaining the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} condition until it [[/act escape dc=21]]{Escapes}. Additionally, it is exposed to the titanic flytrap toxin from the hundreds of tiny hairs that line the inside of its leaves. If the flytrap's jaws Strike was a critical success, the target takes a –2 circumstance penalty to its saving throws against this poison. At the end of each of the target's turns that it remains grabbed, the target takes @Damage[3d6[acid]] damage.

Titanic Flytrap Toxin.name: Titanic Flytrap Toxin
Titanic Flytrap Toxin.description:
Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:21]

Maximum Duration 4 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[2d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[3d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 2} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[4d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed} (1 round)

Scythe Blades.name: Scythe Blades
Scythe Blades.description: Two blades, each hidden in a 15-foot-long ceiling groove, are both connected to a trip wire.
Scythe Blades.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:21|name:Disable a Blade] (trained) to disable each blade

Falling Scythes.name: Falling Scythes
Falling Scythes.description:
Trigger The trip wire is pulled or severed.

Effect Both blades swing down, each one Striking all creatures under the ceiling grooves.

No MAP.name: いいえ MAP/No MAP
No MAP.description: The Scythe Strikes do not apply a MAP.

Second Chance.name: Second Chance
Second Chance.description: Powerful warding magic tied to an object or location tries to regress the ages of a creature and its allies.
Second Chance.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:46|name:Dismantle Spell|traits:action:disable-a-device] (legendary) to take apart the spell one tiny piece at a time, with eyes closed, while recalling every vivid life memory in order, starting from the earliest memory

In the Beginning.name: In the Beginning
In the Beginning.description:
Trigger A creature tries to steal the object or intrude upon the location. If someone uses a proxy or dupe for the theft or intrusion, the trap unerringly targets the true perpetrator or perpetrators at any distance—even across planes.

Effect The triggering creature and up to five coconspirators instantly revert to infants, losing all memories, class abilities, and other skills acquired during their lives (@Check[fortitude|dc:44] negates). Reversing this effect is nearly impossible, requiring powerful magic such as @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.6dDtGIUerazSHIOu]{Wish}.

Frozen Moment.name: Frozen Moment
Frozen Moment.description: Warding magic attempts to trap intruders or would-be thieves in a disrupted time flow.
Frozen Moment.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:38|name:Disassemble Spell Components|traits:action:disable-a-device] (legendary) to rapidly disassemble the spell's myriad components in a single blink of an eye; or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG]{Dispel Magic} (9th rank; counteract DC 36) to counteract the trap before it triggers or to counteract the effect on one creature after the trap is triggered

Adrift in Time.name: Adrift in Time
Adrift in Time.description:
Trigger A creature touches the warded object or area.

Effect The triggering creature and all creatures within 30 feet are trapped in a disrupted time flow (@Check[fortitude|dc:38] negates). The creatures' minds move so quickly that each round seems to last a century, but their bodies and magical energies move so slowly that they can't use any actions except Recall Knowledge. An affected creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:36] saving throw against a warp mind spell immediately and again for every minute of real time that passes while the creature is trapped in the frozen moment. This effect has an unlimited duration but can be counteracted.

Hampering Web.name: Hampering Web
Hampering Web.description: Semitransparent sheets of webbing span the entryway, ready to capture small insects or hamper larger creatures that pass through.

Ensnare.name: Ensnare
Ensnare.description:
Trigger A creature that isn't a spider walks into the web

Effect The web wraps around the triggering creature's body, clinging to their limbs. The triggering creature must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:20] save or take a –10-foot circumstance penalty to all their Speeds until they @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.SkZAQRkLLkmBQNB9]{Escape} the web (DC 20). On a critical failure, the webbing also clings to the creature's face, making them @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}, and they can't attempt to reduce this condition until they Escape the web.

Yellow Mold.name: Yellow Mold
Yellow Mold.disable: @Check[survival|dc:26|name:Remove the Mold] (expert) to remove the mold without triggering the spores

Spore Explosion.name: Spore Explosion
Spore Explosion.description:
Trigger A creature moves into the mold's space or damages the mold. The mold can't use this reaction if it's in direct sunlight or if the damage was fire damage.

Effect The triggering creature and all creatures within @Template[emanation|distance:10]{10 feet} are exposed to yellow mold spores.

Yellow Mold Spores.name: 黄カビの胞子/Yellow Mold Spores
Yellow Mold Spores.description:
Any drained condition from the spores persists after the poison's duration ends.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:26]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[1d8[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[2d8[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 2} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[3d8[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 3} (1 round)

Telekinetic Swarm Trap.name: Telekinetic Swarm Trap
Telekinetic Swarm Trap.description: 念動術の魔力を宿した無害に見える飾りが三つ、部屋の物や部屋そのものの破片を引き寄せて回転する破片の雲にし、部屋のすべての生物を攻撃する.
Telekinetic Swarm Trap.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:27|name:Take Apart Cloud|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to take apart a telekinetic cloud, @Check[thievery|dc:32|name:Disable Telekinetic Decoration|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to disable each telekinetic decoration, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG]{Dispel Magic} (6th rank; counteract DC 30) to counteract each telekinetic decoration
Telekinetic Swarm Trap.reset: The trap deactivates and resets 10 minutes after it has no target creatures (because the creatures either moved too far away or died).
Telekinetic Swarm Trap.routine:
(9 actions) Each decoration uses 3 of the trap's actions each turn, and the trap loses 3 actions each turn for every decoration that is disabled. A decoration uses its first action to move its cloud of objects up to 200 feet, its second action to make the objects Strike, and its third action to add more objects to the cloud, increasing its damage by 1d12 (to a maximum of 4d12+10). If a decoration's cloud is already at maximum damage, it does nothing with its third action.

If a decoration's cloud has been destroyed, the decoration instead spends its first action to create a new cloud of objects inside the room (using the starting damage value) and then its second and third actions to have the cloud move and attack.

Agitate.name: Agitate
Agitate.description:
Trigger A creature stays in the room for at least 6 seconds.

Effect Each telekinetic decoration constructs a cloud of objects in the room (three clouds total) and the trap rolls initiative. The creatures in the room when the trap is triggered become the trap's targets, regardless of whether they leave the room or other creatures later enter the room. Each decoration targets a different creature if possible. A target creature that moves at least 1 mile from the trap ceases being a target, at which point the decoration designates a new target.

Poisonous Mold.name: Poisonous Mold
Poisonous Mold.description: This grotesque fungus releases poisonous spores when a creature comes near.
Poisonous Mold.reset: Unless entirely eradicated (typically with acid or fire), the mold regrows over the course of 2 weeks.

Feed on Decay.name: Feed on Decay
Feed on Decay.description: The mold expands when fed by blood or decay. If at any point a creature takes bleed damage while adjacent to the mold or a dying or dead body is adjacent to the mold, the mold grows to expand into every square adjacent to its current space. This can happen only once per day.

Spore Burst.name: Spore Burst
Spore Burst.description:
Frequency once per day

Trigger A creature touches the mold, hits it, or damages it

Effect The mold explodes in a burst of spores and mold clusters, dealing @Damage[(2d6+6)[poison]] damage to creatures within 10 feet of it, with a @Check[fortitude|dc:18|basic] save.

Floating Spores.name: Floating Spores
Floating Spores.description: 5 feet. A creature that enters or starts its turn in the aura takes @Damage[2d6[poison]] damage.

Hidden Pit.name: Hidden Pit
Hidden Pit.description: A wooden trapdoor covers a pit that's 10 feet square and 20 feet deep.
Hidden Pit.stealth: (or 0 if the trapdoor is disabled or broken)
Hidden Pit.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:12|name:Remove the Trapdoor] to remove the trapdoor
Hidden Pit.reset: Creatures can still fall into the trap, but the trapdoor must be reset manually for the trap to become hidden again.

Pitfall.name: Pitfall
Pitfall.description:
Trigger A creature walks onto the trapdoor.

Effect The triggering creature falls in and takes falling damage (typically @Damage[10[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage). That creature can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.3yoajuKjwHZ9ApUY]{Grab an Edge} reaction to avoid falling.

Armageddon Orb.name: Armageddon Orb
Armageddon Orb.description: A roiling red orb, forged from a drop of the god Rovagug's blood, rains fire from the sky when a specified condition is met.
Armageddon Orb.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:48|name:Imbue Thieves' Tools|traits:action:disable-a-device] (legendary) to imbue a thieves' toolkit with aspects representing Asmodeus and Sarenrae and use them to drain away the orb's power over 10 minutes; the character attempting this check takes @Damage[5[fire]] damage each round until the orb is depleted

Burn It All.name: Burn It All
Burn It All.description:
Trigger A special condition set by the trap's creator occurs, typically the event of their death.

Effect Fire rains from the sky in a 100-mile radius, dealing @Damage[10d6[fire]] damage to creatures and objects in the area. Each creature or object can attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:46|basic] save. Any creature reduced to 0 Hit Points by this damage dies instantly. This is not enough damage to completely burn away a forest or level an entire mountain or city, but it typically kills most creatures in the area.

Quicksand.name: Quicksand
Quicksand.description: A 15-foot-wide patch of water and sand attempts to submerge creatures that step onto it.
Quicksand.stealth: (trained) (or -10 and no minimum proficiency if the surface is disturbed)
Quicksand.disable: @Check[survival|dc:18|name:Safely Disturb the Surface] (trained) to disturb the surface
Quicksand.reset: The hazard still submerges anyone who walks in, but the surface doesn't become hidden again until it settles over the course of 24 hours.
Quicksand.routine: (1 action) On its initiative, the quicksand pulls down each creature within it. A creature that was submerged up to its waist becomes submerged up to its neck, and a creature that was submerged up to its neck is pulled under and has to hold its breath to avoid suffocation. A creature in the quicksand can attempt a @Check[athletics|dc:20|traits:action:swim] check to Swim to either raise itself by one step if it's submerged to its neck or worse, or to move 5 feet if it's submerged only up to its waist. On a critical failure, the creature is pulled down one step. A creature that Swims out of the quicksand escapes the hazard and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} in a space adjacent to the quicksand patch. Other creatures can Aid the creature, typically by using a rope or similar aid, or attempt to pull the creature out with their own @Check[athletics|dc:20|name:Aid Another Creature in Quicksand] check, with the same results as if the creature attempted the check.

Submerge.name: Submerge
Submerge.description:
Trigger A Huge or smaller creature walks onto the quicksand

Effect The triggering creature sinks into the quicksand up to its waist. The quicksand rolls initiative if it hasn't already.

Ghostly Choir.name: Ghostly Choir
Ghostly Choir.description: A choir of lost souls rises out of the floor, singing an eerie chant that terrifies its listeners and buffets their bodies with walls of sound.
Ghostly Choir.disable: @Check[performance|dc:28|name:Sing a Different Tune|traits:haunt] (trained) to disrupt the song's resonance with another tune or @Check[religion|dc:28|name:Ritually Silence the Haunt|traits:haunt] (trained) to ritually silence the spirits

Profane Chant.name: 冒涜の詠唱/Profane Chant
Profane Chant.description:
Trigger A creature moves within 10 feet of the section of floor from which the choir can arise

Effect The choir rises, and its song deals @Damage[(4d8+18)[mental]] damage to living creatures within 30 feet of the souls' spectral forms. Affected creatures must each attempt a @Check[will|dc:24|traits:damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature takes full damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage. It also becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing} for 1 round.

Flensing Blades.name: Flensing Blades
Flensing Blades.description: A whirling tornado of spectrally propelled glass and steel slices whatever it touches to ribbons.
Flensing Blades.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:35|name:Adjust Blades|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to precisely adjust the blades so that they destroy each other, or @Check[religion|dc:38|name:Weaken the Haunt] (expert) to weaken the haunt; four successes are required to disable it
Flensing Blades.reset: The haunt draws jagged shards back into its area over the course of an hour, after which it can trigger again.
Flensing Blades.routine:
(3 actions) The tornado of blades uses 3 actions to move, traveling up to 30 feet with each action and dealing @Damage[(2d10+10)[slashing]] damage. Each creature in its path must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:33].

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage plus @Damage[1d10[bleed]].

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and @Damage[1d10[bleed]]. It also becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Yl48xTdMh3aeQYL2]{Wounded 1} (or increases its wounded value by 1, if it is already wounded).

Each successful check to disable this hazard reduces the haunt's movement by 30 feet, and the fourth success disables it completely.

Whirling Blades.name: Whirling Blades
Whirling Blades.description:
Trigger Three or more creatures enter the area of the haunt

Effect Sharp fragments lift up from the ground and begin to spin in rapid circles taking up one 5-foot square. The haunt rolls initiative.

Plummeting Doom.name: 墜落の破滅/Plummeting Doom
Plummeting Doom.description: Four vengeful spirits grab interlopers and toss them off the edge of a nearby 120-foot-tall cliff.
Plummeting Doom.stealth: (master) to hear the echoes of a faraway object crashing into the ground
Plummeting Doom.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:40|name:Forcefully Push Spirits Back] (trained) to push back so forcefully that the spirits fear being thrown off the cliff, or @Check[religion|dc:40|name:Seal the Spirits] (expert) to temporarily seal the spirits away

Call of the Ground.name: Call of the Ground
Call of the Ground.description:
Trigger A creature approaches within 15 feet of the edge of the cliff.

Effect Each spirit attempts to throw a creature within 60 feet of the cliff's edge off the cliff by attempting a check with a +26 modifier against the target's Fortitude DC. If the haunt succeeds, the target is thrown over the edge of the cliff, where it then falls 120 feet to the ground. Until the haunt is defeated, each creature within 60 feet of the cliff's edge must attempt a @Check[will|dc:40] save each time they would spend an action or reaction to Arrest a Fall, Fly, Grab an Edge, or otherwise avoid falling; if they fail the save, the action is disrupted. The haunt automatically attempts to counteract spells that would slow a fall or mitigate the effects of falling, such as feather fall, with a counteract modifier of +32.

Treacherous Scree.name: Treacherous Scree
Treacherous Scree.description: この傾斜地の足場は一見安定しているように見えるが、表面を覆う小石は緩く詰まっており滑りやすい.
Treacherous Scree.disable: @Check[survival|dc:20|name:Navigate Safe Path] (trained) to navigate a safe path

Rockslide.name: Rockslide
Rockslide.description:
Trigger A creature steps on the unstable ground.

Effect Rocks tumble and slip beneath its feet. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:21|traits:damaging-effect] save as they tumble against the rocks, which deal @Damage[(2d10+13)[bludgeoning]] damage.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage and falls prone in its space.

Failure The creature takes full damage, falls prone in its space, and tumbles down to the bottom of the slope.

Critical Failure As failure, but double damage.

Wheel Of Misery.name: Wheel Of Misery
Wheel Of Misery.description: An ornate wheel set into a wall-divided into six segments with colored runes on each-is controlled by a magical sensor that detects any creature within 100 feet in front of it.
Wheel Of Misery.stealth:
(熟練)で魔法のセンサーを感知できる；車輪がDCを0

持っていることに気づく
Wheel Of Misery.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:26|name:Stop Wheel from Spinning|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) on the wheel to stop it from spinning, @Check[thievery|dc:22|name:Erase Rune|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to erase each rune, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG]{Dispel Magic} (4th rank; counteract DC 22) to counteract each rune
Wheel Of Misery.reset: The trap deactivates and resets if 1 minute passes without any creature moving within range of its sensor.
Wheel Of Misery.routine:
(2 actions) On its initiative, the trap uses its first action to spin, then stops. Roll 1d6 to determine which segment is topmost when the wheel stops spinning. The wheel uses its second action to replicate the spell listed for that segment (3rd rank, DC 24, spell attack modifier +14). This spell's target is centered on or otherwise includes the nearest creature in the area. This increases the spell's range to 100 feet if necessary. Any spell cast by this trap is arcane.

1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.o4lRVTwSxnOOn5vl]{Sleep}

2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.DCQHaLrYXMI37dvW]{Paralyze}

3 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9AAkVUCwF6WVNNY2]{Lightning Bolt} (100-foot line)

4 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.VosLNn2M8S7JH67D]{Blindness}

5 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.thAHF1zxNplLCJPO]{Caustic Blast}

6 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.J7Y7tl0bbdz7TcCc]{Enfeeble}

Wheel Spin.name: Wheel Spin
Wheel Spin.description:
Trigger A creature enters the sensor's detection area.

Effect The wheel begins to spin and rolls initiative.

Polymorph Trap.name: ポリモーフ トラップ/Polymorph Trap
Polymorph Trap.description: A Druidic glyph tries to transforms a trespasser into an animal.
Polymorph Trap.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:32|name:Drain Glyph's Power|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to drain the glyph's power harmlessly, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG]{Dispel Magic} (6th rank; counteract DC 30) to counteract the glyph

Cursed Metamorphosis.name: Cursed Metamorphosis
Cursed Metamorphosis.description:
Trigger A creature moves within 30 feet of the glyph without speaking the passphrase in Druidic

Effect The creature is targeted by @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.dN8QBNuTiaBHCKUe]{Cursed Metamorphosis} (@Check[will|dc:32] save).

Spinning Blade Pillar.name: 回転刃の柱/Spinning Blade Pillar
Spinning Blade Pillar.description: A metal pole with three razor-sharp spinning blades is hidden in the floor, connected to trigger plates in up to eight floor tiles and a hidden control panel within 30 feet.
Spinning Blade Pillar.stealth: (trained) or @Check[perception|dc:26|traits:action:seek] (expert) to notice the control panel
Spinning Blade Pillar.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:21|name:Disable Pillar|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) twice on the pillar, or @Check[thievery|dc:19|name:Disable Trap (Control Panel)|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) once on the control panel deactivates the whole trap. Breaking the control panel prevents anyone from disabling the trap using the control panel and prevents the trap from deactivating automatically.
Spinning Blade Pillar.reset: The trap deactivates and resets after 1 minute.
Spinning Blade Pillar.routine: (3 actions) The trap uses its first action to make a spinning blade Strike against each adjacent creature, its second action to move straight in a random direction (roll [[/gmr 1d4 #Direction]] to determine the direction), and its third to make a spinning blade Strike against each adjacent creature. This trap doesn't take a multiple attack penalty.

Rising Pillar.name: 上昇する柱/Rising Pillar
Rising Pillar.description:
Trigger A creature steps on one of the trapped floor tiles

Effect The trap pops up in a grid intersection and makes a spinning blade Strike against one adjacent creature (if any), then rolls initiative.

Jealous Abjurer.name: Jealous Abjurer
Jealous Abjurer.description: A robe-clad spirit rises out of the floor, pointing an accusing finger.
Jealous Abjurer.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:36|name:Convince the Spirit], @Check[nature|dc:36|name:Convince the Spirit], @Check[occultism|dc:36|name:Convince the Spirit], or @Check[religion|dc:36|name:Convince the Spirit] (master) to convince the spirit that the target's magical knowledge is too great to be trifled with

Rend Magic.name: Rend Magic
Rend Magic.description:
Trigger A creature that is currently affected by a beneficial spell approaches within 30 feet of the abjurer.

Effect Envying the fame and magical prowess of others, the jealous abjurer attempts to engineer a catastrophic failure in the highest-level beneficial spell currently affecting its target. It attempts a counteract check with a +26 modifier. If the counteract check succeeds, the spell is dispelled, and the creature it had been affecting takes @Damage[(4d12+30)[force]] damage as the spell violently implodes (@Check[reflex|dc:32|basic] save).

Vorpal Executioner.name: ヴォーパルの処刑人/Vorpal Executioner
Vorpal Executioner.description: A wickedly sharp saw blade descends and travels along grooves in a complex path throughout the room, attempting to decapitate everyone within.
Vorpal Executioner.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:41|name:Jam Saw Blade|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) at four different junctions to jam all the saw blade's possible paths, preventing it from traveling through the room
Vorpal Executioner.reset: The trap resets over the course of the round and can be triggered again 1 round later.

Total Decapitation.name: Total Decapitation
Total Decapitation.description:
Trigger A creature attempts to exit the room.

Effect The saw blade travels along its path, making one Strike against each creature in the room, twisting and varying its height for a maximum chance of beheading its targets.

Decapitation.name: Decapitation
Decapitation.description: On a critical hit, a target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:39|traits:damaging-effect] save or be decapitated, dying instantly unless it can survive without a head.

No MAP.name: No MAP
No MAP.description: The saw blade Strikes do not apply a MAP.

Perilous Flash Flood.name: 危険な突発洪水/Perilous Flash Flood
Perilous Flash Flood.description: Whether made up of water rushing through the streets that sweeps up dangerous debris along the way or a less natural substance bursting free of its container, this relentless flood batters everything in its path.
Perilous Flash Flood.disable: Three @Check[athletics|dc:35|name:Move Barricade], @Check[crafting|dc:35|name:Construct Barricade], or @Check[survival|dc:35|name:Construct Barricade] checks to move or construct barricades strong enough to create a shelter from the flood. While this creates a safe place to stand, creatures outside of the barricaded area may still be in danger depending on the nature and the source of the flood.
Perilous Flash Flood.routine:
(1 action) The flood advances forward 60 feet, crashing into all creatures within its area. Each creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:30] save as the floodwaters pummel them and pull them downstream. The amount and type of damage dealt are based on the nature of the flood, and certain types of floods impose additional effects. The turbulent waters mean creatures within the area of the flood must attempt a @Check[athletics|dc:20|name:Swim|traits:action:swim] check to Swim in order to move, and those who do not succeed at a check to Swim each round may drown.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is moved 10 feet along with the water.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and is moved 20 feet along with the water.

  • Acidic Runoff This caustic flood dissolves flesh as it moves, dealing @Damage[1d12[bludgeoning],(1d12+8)[acid]]{1d12 bludgeoning damage and 1d12+8 acid damage}. Additionally, it deals @Damage[2d6[persistent,acid]] damage to creatures who critically fail their Fortitude saves.
  • Battering Waves This flood of rushing water deals @Damage[(2d12+10)[bludgeoning]] damage.
  • Repulsive Refuse This flood has picked up tainted or disease-ridden objects like sewer runoff or rotting food. It deals @Damage[(2d12+8)[bludgeoning]] damage. Each creature exposed to the flood must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:29] save, becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} on a failure or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} on a critical failure. Additionally, creatures who come into contact with the flood waters are exposed to filth fever (@Check[fortitude|dc:20]).
  • Sharp Debris The waters have picked up various objects, some of which are particularly sharp. The flood deals @Damage[1d12[bludgeoning]] damage and @Damage[(1d12+12)[piercing]] damage.
  • Sticky Goo The substance is particularly sticky. It deals @Damage[(2d12+6)[bludgeoning]] damage. Additionally, each creature in the flood must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:29] save at the beginning of their turn each round. On a failed saving throw, they take a –10-foot circumstance penalty to all their Speeds for 1 round. On a critical failure, they are instead @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} for 1 round.

Burst Free.name: Burst Free
Burst Free.description:
Trigger A creature or effect breaks the flood's containment.

Effect The hazard rolls initiative as the flood surges forth.

Filth Fever.name: Filth Fever
Filth Fever.description:
The sickened and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} conditions from filth fever don't improve on their own until the disease is cured.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:20]

Stage 1 carrier with no ill effect ([[/gmr 1d4 #hours]]{1d4 hours})

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} (1 day)

Stage 3 sickened 1 and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} as long as it remains sickened (1 day)

Stage 4 unconscious (1 day)

Stage 5 dead

Green Slime.name: 緑のスライム/Green Slime
Green Slime.description: A caustic green film clings to the ceiling above, watching for prey to pass beneath it.
Green Slime.disable: @Check[survival|dc:33|name:Peel the Slime Off the Ceiling] (expert) to carefully peel the slime off the ceiling without touching it
Green Slime.reset: 1 hour, as the slime feasts and then slowly creeps back up to the ceiling

Poisoned Dart Gallery.name: Poisoned Dart Gallery
Poisoned Dart Gallery.description: Countless holes to launch poison darts from line a long hallway with a hidden control panel on the far end.
Poisoned Dart Gallery.hp: to destroy the control panel and disable the trap
Poisoned Dart Gallery.stealth: (expert) or @Check[perception|dc:31|traits:action:seek] (master) to notice the control panel
Poisoned Dart Gallery.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:21|name:Disable Trap (Control Panel)|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) on the control panel deactivates the trap.
Poisoned Dart Gallery.reset: The trap deactivates and resets after 1 minute.
Poisoned Dart Gallery.routine: (1 action) The trap launches one dart against every creature in the gallery as 1 action. Because it launches darts continuously, the trap can also use the Continuous Barrage free action (see below) to launch darts at each creature during that creature's turn.

Dart Volley.name: Dart Volley
Dart Volley.description:
Trigger A creature enters the hallway or ends its turn in the hallway

Effect The trap makes a poisoned dart Strike against the triggering creature, then rolls initiative.

Continuous Barrage.name: Continuous Barrage
Continuous Barrage.description:
Trigger A creature within the active gallery finishes an action

Effect The trap makes a poisoned dart Strike against the triggering creature.

Flesset Poison.name: Flesset 毒/Flesset Poison
Flesset Poison.description:
Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:22]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[1d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[2d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[3d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 3} (1 round)

No MAP.name: No MAP
No MAP.description: The poisoned dart Strikes do not apply a MAP.

Pharaoh's Ward.name: Pharaoh's Ward
Pharaoh's Ward.description: A curse is imbued on an entryway's threshold.
Pharaoh's Ward.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:27|name:Remove the Lintel] (master) to painstakingly remove the lintel without triggering the magic, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG]{Dispel Magic} (4th rank; counteract DC 25) to counteract the rune
Pharaoh's Ward.reset: The trap resets when the door is shut.

Curse the Intruders.name: Curse the Intruders
Curse the Intruders.description:
Trigger The seal on the tomb is broken from the outside.

Effect Each living creature within 60 feet must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:23] save or be subjected to the pharaoh's curse. A cursed creature takes a -2 status penalty to Fortitude saves, and any natural or magical healing it receives is halved. The curse remains until removed by @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.SUKaxVZW2TlM8lu0]{Cleanse Affliction} or similar magic.

Banshee's Symphony.name: Banshee's Symphony
Banshee's Symphony.description: A magically contagious wail of the banshee spell is trapped in the larynx of an invisible, mummified elf.
Banshee's Symphony.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:42|name:Pierce Larynx|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to pierce the invisible larynx so precisely that the magic releases in a trickle before the trap activates, @Check[thievery|dc:44|name:Deconstruct Larynx|traits:action:disable-a-device] (legendary) three times to deconstruct the larynx while the trap is active in such a way that it tears the spell apart, or spell DC 38 (9th level) to counteract the wail of the banshee before the trap activates
Banshee's Symphony.routine: (1 行動) 罠はその行動を使って、前のターンにバンシーの嘆きに対するセーヴに失敗したランダムな1体の生物を、元のものと同じ統計を持つバンシーの嘆きを放たせるよう強制する。たとえその生物が死亡しているか発声できなくても、罠からどれだけ離れていても嘆く。呪文の詠唱とは異なり、この罠のバンシーの嘆きによるドレインは対象のドレイン値を増加させる。任意のセーヴでクリティカル成功した生物は将来のラウンドで罠の影響を受ける可能性はあるが、罠によって嘆かされることはない.

Scream.name: Scream
Scream.description:
Trigger Three or more living creatures remain within 100 feet of the trap for 6 seconds or longer.

Effect The trap releases an arcane wail of the banshee (DC 40) with a 100-foot-radius emanation instead of 40 feet, targeting all living creatures in the area, and rolls initiative.

Insistent Privacy Fence.name: Insistent Privacy Fence
Insistent Privacy Fence.description: A stretch of fence is electrified by a hidden Stasian coil.
Insistent Privacy Fence.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:28|name:Disconnect the coil from the fence|traits:electricity,hazard,trap,mechanical] (熟練)でコイルをフェンスから慎重に切り離す、または@Check[crafting|dc:30|name:Jury-rig a grounding device|traits:electricity,hazard,trap,mechanical] (達人)で応急の接地装置を組み付けてショックを無効化する
Insistent Privacy Fence.reset: The trap deactivates and resets after 1 minute.

Shock.name: Shock
Shock.description:
Trigger A creature touches the fence directly or with a tool or weapon

Effect The fence deals @Damage[7d12[electricity]] damage to the triggering creature (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic|traits:electricity,mechanical,trap,hazard] save).

Summoning Rune.name: Summoning Rune
Summoning Rune.description: A cloud of invisible magical sensors in a 10-foot radius surrounds an invisible wall or floor rune the size of the creature to be summoned.
Summoning Rune.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:15|name:Approach Rune] to approach without triggering the trap followed by @Check[thievery|dc:17|name:Erase Rune|traits:action:disable-a-device] (trained) to erase the rune, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG]{Dispel Magic} (1st rank; counteract DC 15) to counteract the rune
Summoning Rune.reset: The trap resets each day at dawn.

Summon Monster.name: Summon Monster
Summon Monster.description:
Trigger A creature enters the cloud of magical sensors

Effect This trap summons a specific 1st-level creature, determined when the trap is created. The creature rolls initiative and remains for [[/br 2d6 #rounds]]{2d6 rounds}, after which the spell ends and the creature disappears. The creature also disappears if someone disables the trap before the duration expires. The summoned creature can use 3 actions each round and can use reactions, unlike most summoned creatures.

Eternal Flame.name: Eternal Flame
Eternal Flame.description: A raging spectral inferno arises out of thin air, strengthening all undead creatures within its area. This haunt most often arises from the charred remains of a group of three people who burned to death, whether in a terrible accident or a deliberate execution, and their unavenged souls burn with rage.
Eternal Flame.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:27|name:Calm the Spirit's Rage] (expert) to temporarily calm the rage of one of the three spirits, or @Check[religion|dc:30|name:Exorcise Spirit] (trained) to exorcise one of the spirits; three total successes are required to disable the haunt
Eternal Flame.reset: その炎はすべての生きている生物が区域を離れてから1分で消えるが、1時間後には怒りと痛みが再び煮え返り、怨念は再び発動する準備が整う.
Eternal Flame.routine: (1 action) Phantom flames rage across the haunted area, dealing @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage to each living creature within the area (@Check[will|dc:23|basic] save). Undead creatures in the area are infused with flames for the following round. They gain the fire trait and immunity to fire, and all their attacks deal an additional @Damage[1d6[fire]] damage. Objects in the area are unaffected.

Searing Agony.name: Searing Agony
Searing Agony.description:
Trigger A living creature approaches within 10 feet of the remains of a victim of the original fire

Effect Memories of the pain suffered by the fire's past victims assault the triggering creature's mind. The creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:25] save, and the haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 3}, and it is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} for as long as it remains within the haunt's area and for 3 rounds thereafter.

Drowning Pit.name: Drowning Pit
Drowning Pit.description: A trapdoor covers a 10-foot-square pit that's 30 feet deep and has 5 feet of water at the bottom. Four water spouts in the walls connect to hidden water tanks. Each water spout extends out of a different wall, 6 inches from the top of the pit.
Drowning Pit.stealth: (trained); @Check[perception|dc:22|traits:action:seek] (expert) to notice the water spouts once the pit opens
Drowning Pit.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18] (trained) to seal each water spout, @Check[thievery|dc:22] (trained) to open the trapdoor, or @Check[athletics|dc:22] to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.action-macros.Macro.yMTKMnaYSGtDz4wk]{Force Open: Athletics} the trapdoor
Drowning Pit.reset: The trap can be reset if the door is manually reengaged and the water tanks are refilled; it can be reset without draining the pit, but doing so renders the pit less effective.
Drowning Pit.routine: (4 actions) The trap loses 1 action each turn for each disabled water spout. On each of the trap's actions, a spout pours water, increasing the depth of the water by 5 feet. Once the pit is full of water, the pit stops using actions, but creatures in the pit begin drowning.

Pitfall.name: Pitfall
Pitfall.description:
Trigger A creature walks onto the trapdoor

Effect The triggering creature falls in and takes damage from the fall, reduced by 5 feet for falling into the water (typically @Damage[12[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage). A creature can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.3yoajuKjwHZ9ApUY]{Grab an Edge} to avoid falling. The trapdoor then slams shut, and the hazard rolls initiative.

Lava Flume Tube.name: Lava Flume Tube
Lava Flume Tube.description: 石に彫られた四つの門付きの水路が溶岩を15-フィートの高さの部屋へ流し込み、床は引き下げて硬化した溶岩を下の室へ落とすことができる.
Lava Flume Tube.hp: to destroy a channel gate (this prevents that channel from being disabled and stops the trap from resetting); Floor Hardness 18; Floor HP 72 (BT 36)
Lava Flume Tube.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:29|name:Block a Channel|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to block a channel, or @Check[thievery|dc:31|name:Release Floor Latch|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to release the floor latch and escape to the chamber below
Lava Flume Tube.reset: The trap deactivates and resets after 1 hour by withdrawing the floor, cracking and dumping the hardened lava (and any creatures trapped inside) into the chamber. Creatures fall 40 feet, taking falling damage (typically @Damage[17[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage).
Lava Flume Tube.routine:
(4 actions) The trap loses 1 action per disabled channel each turn. On each action, a different channel spews lava, dealing @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage to each creature within 10 feet of the channel (@Check[reflex|dc:27|basic] save), and increasing the depth of the lava in the room by 1 foot (4 feet per round if all the channels are active).

A creature that starts its turn in lava takes @Damage[8d6[fire]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} until it @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.SkZAQRkLLkmBQNB9]{Escapes} the hardening lava (DC 27). The creature might suffocate if covered in lava.

Lava from the previous round hardens fully at the start of the trap's turn, effectively raising the floor of the room. Once the room is full of lava, the trap stops taking actions, but creatures in the room remain stuck until the floor opens and the trap resets.

Flume Activation.name: Flume Activation
Flume Activation.description:
Trigger A creature tries to leave the room.

Effect The exits seal instantly and the trap rolls initiative.

Web Lurker Noose.name: Web Lurker Noose
Web Lurker Noose.description: Discreet webbing at throat level snags a creature that walks into it.
Web Lurker Noose.disable: @Check[survival|dc:18|name:Rearrange the Webbing|traits:mechanical,trap] (trained) or @Check[thievery|dc:18|name:Rearrange the Webbing|traits:mechanical,trap] (expert) to rearrange the webbing.

Web Noose.name: クモの巣の輪/Web Noose
Web Noose.description:
Trigger A creature steps into the square with the web tripwire.

Effect The web lurker noose makes a noose Strike against the triggering creature.

Steam Vents.name: Steam Vents
Steam Vents.description: Large pressurized pipes connected to a trip wire are rigged to release a blast of steam on trespassers.
Steam Vents.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22|name:Disconnect the trip wire|traits:mechanical,trap,hazard]{Thievery (trained)} to disconnect the trip wire or a @Check[crafting|dc:24|name:Reduce the pressure|traits:mechanical,trap,hazard]{Crafting (expert)} to carefully reduce the pressure and prevent the blast

Steam Blast.name: Steam Blast
Steam Blast.description:
Trigger The trip wire is pulled or severed, typically because a creature walked through the square with the trip wire

Effect Steam erupts from the pipes, dealing @Damage[3d6[bludgeoning],3d6[fire]]{3d6 bludgeoning damage and 3d6 fire damage} (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic|name:Avoid the erupting steam|traits:mechanical,trap,hazard]) to all creatures within 15 feet. Creatures that critically fail their save are knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}.

Brown Mold.name: Brown Mold
Brown Mold.description: この一見平凡な菌は空気中の熱を吸い取る.
Brown Mold.disable: @Check[survival|dc:18|name:Remove Brown Mold|traits:environmental,fungus] (trained) to safely remove the mold
Brown Mold.reset: After expanding, the brown mold can't grow again for 1 day.

Emit Cold.name: Emit Cold
Emit Cold.description: 5 feet. Brown mold deals @Damage[2d6[cold]] damage to nearby creatures.

Leech Warmth.name: Leech Warmth
Leech Warmth.description:
Trigger Fire comes within 5 feet of the brown mold.

Effect The brown mold expands into every square adjacent to its space. As it grows, it pulls more heat from its surroundings, dealing @Damage[(2d6+6)[cold]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:18|basic] save) to creatures within 10 feet after it expands.

Confounding Betrayal.name: Confounding Betrayal
Confounding Betrayal.description: Allies appear to shed their disguises and reveal themselves to be malevolent monsters.
Confounding Betrayal.disable: @Check[deception|dc:28|name:Confound the Haunt] (expert) twice to confound the haunt with your own deceptions, or @Check[occultism|dc:28|name:Create a Ward Against Influence] (trained) twice to create a ward against the haunt's mental influence
Confounding Betrayal.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the area but resets immediately thereafter.
Confounding Betrayal.routine:
(1 action; illusion, incapacitation, occult) The haunt continues to confound victims' senses and inspire them to commit violence against each other. Each creature in the haunt's area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:26] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and temporarily immune for 1 minute.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature is confused for 1 minute.

Unmask.name: Unmask
Unmask.description:
Trigger Two or more creatures enter the haunt's area

Effect Each creature sees the forms of nearby creatures shift and change, appearing to transform into fiendish or aberrant beings with a thirst for blood. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:30] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature sees through the illusions entirely and is temporarily immune to the haunt's routine for 1 minute.

Success The creature is unaffected by the strange images.

Failure The creature believes the illusions to be true; if they become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} by the haunt's routine, they can't attempt flat checks to end the confused condition when they take damage.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature is left with a lingering suspicion of others and can't benefit from Aid reactions for 24 hours.

Grasp Of The Damned.name: Grasp Of The Damned
Grasp Of The Damned.description: These desperate spirits are the echoes of people who committed great atrocities in the name of an evil god. Now, they are left with only the knowledge that their souls have been damned, and the unwavering belief that they can better their fate by providing powerful sacrifices for their fiendish masters.
Grasp Of The Damned.disable: @Check[religion|dc:46|name:Call For Divine Intervention] (master) to inspire a deity to intervene and counteract the ritual

Mark for Damnation.name: Mark for Damnation
Mark for Damnation.description:
Trigger Three or more sentient living creatures of 13th level or higher enter the haunt's area.

Effect The haunt deals @Damage[(6d12+35)[void]] damage to each creature in the haunt's area, and each creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:40|traits:damaging-effect] save.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage and is doomed 1.

Success The creature takes half damage and is doomed 1.

Failure The creature takes full damage, becomes doomed 2, and is marked for damnation.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage, becomes doomed 3, and is marked for damnation.

If a creature that is marked for damnation dies within the next 24 hours, including from the haunt's damage, its soul is immediately dragged away to the plane of the evil deity that the damned spirits served, where the creature's soul is held captive by one of that deity's powerful servitors. Only wish and similarly potent effects are able to recover the lost soul directly; however, it is also possible to recover the soul by journeying to the evil plane and defeating the soul's captor.

Bloodthirsty Urge.name: Bloodthirsty Urge
Bloodthirsty Urge.description: An object haunted by the echoes of a vicious mind attempts to kill someone who comes near.
Bloodthirsty Urge.disable: @Check[religion|dc:29|name:Exorcise Spirit] (master) to exorcise the spirit or @Check[diplomacy|dc:31|name:Talk Down the Spirit] (expert) to talk it down

Quietus.name: Quietus
Quietus.description:
Trigger A creature moves within 10 feet of the haunted object

Effect The haunt takes control of the triggering creature, forcing it to attack itself. The creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:29] save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target makes a Strike against itself and automatically hits; the target also becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The target makes a Strike against itself and automatically scores a critical hit; the target also becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The target attempts a Fortitude save. If the target succeeds, it is subject to the effects of a failure instead. If the target fails, it is reduced to 0 HP and dies

Hammer Of Forbiddance.name: Hammer Of Forbiddance
Hammer Of Forbiddance.description: An enormous hammer at an edifice's entrance swings down in an attempt to damage a creature entering an area, push it back, and prevent it from going any further.
Hammer Of Forbiddance.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:28|name:Prevent Hammer from Swinging|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) once on the hammer itself and once on its joint to prevent the hammer from swinging
Hammer Of Forbiddance.reset: 罠はラウンドの間にリセットされ、1 ラウンド後に再び振り下ろす準備ができる.

Forbid Entry.name: Forbid Entry
Forbid Entry.description:
Trigger A creature attempts to enter through the entrance.

Effect The hammer swings down, making a Strike against the triggering creature.

Hammer.name: Hammer
Hammer.description: On a hit, the target is knocked back 10 feet and must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:30] or be unable to enter the edifice through any entrance for 24 hours (on a critical hit, the target automatically fails the Will save); no multiple attack penalty

Hallucination Powder Trap.name: Hallucination Powder Trap
Hallucination Powder Trap.description: A tube of hallucinogenic powder armed with a miniature explosive is connected to a doorknob or similar latch.
Hallucination Powder Trap.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:26|name:Disable Hammer|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to disable the hammer that strikes the percussion cap

Powder Burst.name: Powder Burst
Powder Burst.description:
Trigger The latch is opened or the tube is broken.

Effect The tube explodes, spraying hallucinogenic powder in a @Template[cone|distance:30]. Any creature in the cone must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:24] save or be confused for 1 round and take a -2 status penalty to Perception checks and saves against mental effects for [[/gmr 1d4 #hours]]{1d4 hours}. On a critical failure, the penalty is instead -4.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.6OpwHz0f55wPawHI]{Effect: Powder Burst}

Spectral Reflection.name: Spectral Reflection
Spectral Reflection.description: The reflection in the mirror subtly twists and distorts, its expression taking on an unnerving sneer of malice.
Spectral Reflection.disable: @Check[religion|dc:23|name:Exorcise Spirit] (訓練済み)で霊を祓うか、または@Check[thievery|dc:23|name:Cover the Mirror|traits:action:disable-a-device] (訓練済み)で鏡を素早く覆う
Spectral Reflection.reset: The haunt re-forms after 1 minute, manifesting in any mirror within its infused area (see Special below).

Spectral Impale.name: 幽霊の串刺し/Spectral Impale
Spectral Impale.description:
Trigger A living creature approaches within 15 feet of the mirror, and the mirror is lit with dim or brighter light.

Effect Shadowy barbs impale the body of the reflected creature as the haunt makes a shadow barbs Strike.

Sap Vitality.name: Sap Vitality
Sap Vitality.description: A creature hit by the reflection's shadow barbs must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:22] save as the haunt tries to draw a portion of its vital essence into the mirror. The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} on a failed saving throw, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 2} on a critical failure.

Special.name: Special
Special.description: Spectral reflections often infuse entire buildings, manifesting in any sufficiently large mirror. The example Hit Points and Hardness given represent a typical mirror reinforced by the haunt; at your discretion, the haunt might appear in reflective surfaces that are harder to destroy. Defeating a manifestation through damage destroys the surface, preventing the haunt from using it again. If this haunt appears in an area with many mirrors, consider giving PCs double or even triple the XP for a typical simple hazard.

Poisoned Lock.name: Poisoned Lock
Poisoned Lock.description: A spring-loaded, poisoned spine is hidden near the keyhole of a lock. Disabling or breaking the trap does not disable or break the lock.
Poisoned Lock.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:17|name:Disarm Lock Trap|traits:mechanical,trap] (trained) on the spring mechanism

Spring.name: Spring
Spring.description:
Trigger A creature tries to unlock or Pick the Lock.

Effect A spine extends to Strike the triggering creature.

Snowfall.name: 降雪/Snowfall
Snowfall.description: Loose snow and ice have built up on a high surface, such as a tree branch or a rooftop. Its grip on the surface is tenuous, and it is likely to fall if the surface moves.
Snowfall.disable: @Check[survival|dc:19|name:Safely Dislodge the Snow] (trained) to safely dislodge the snow, or deal any amount of fire damage to destroy the hazard without triggering it

Snowdrop.name: Snowdrop
Snowdrop.description:
Trigger A creature moves beneath where the snowfall is resting.

Effect Ice and snow fall on the triggering creature, dealing @Damage[(2d6+3)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:18|basic] save) and soaking their clothing. Until they change into fresh clothing or spend at least an hour in an area of normal or higher temperature, they treat cold environments as one step colder (for example, mild cold as severe cold).

Spear Launcher.name: Spear Launcher
Spear Launcher.description: A wall socket loaded with a spear connects to a floor tile in one 5-foot square.
Spear Launcher.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18|name:Disable Thievery] (trained) on the floor tile or wall socket

Spear.name: Spear
Spear.description:
Trigger Pressure is applied to the floor tile.

Effect The trap shoots a spear, making a Strike against the creature or object on the floor tile.

Shrieker.name: Shrieker
Shrieker.description: This human-sized purple mushroom emits a piercing shriek when disturbed.
Shrieker.disable: @Check[survival|dc:18|name:Disable Air Sac|traits:environmental,fungus] to carefully approach and cut the mushroom's air sac without triggering the shrieker

Shriek.name: Shriek
Shriek.description:
Trigger A creature or light source approaches within 10 feet of the shrieker or the shrieker takes damage.

Effect The shrieker emits a deafening screech that deals @Damage[1d6[sonic]] damage to creatures within @Template[emanation|distance:30]{30 feet} (@Check[fortitude|dc:16|basic|traits:auditory] save; creatures that critically fail this saving throw are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.9PR9y0bi4JPKnHPR]{Deafened} for 1 minute).

Gravehall Trap.name: Gravehall Trap
Gravehall Trap.description: When a living creature crosses the midpoint of the trapped tunnel, necromantic magic partially animates the corpses buried in the walls and ceiling.
Gravehall Trap.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:24|name:Disrupt Magical Trigger|traits:action:disable-a-device] (expert) to disrupt the magical trigger before the hazard rolls initiative, or @Check[religion|dc:18|name:Overwhelm the Area with Vitality Energy] (trained) to overwhelm the area with vitality energy and prevent the hazard from using its routine for 1 round; on the third successful Religion check, the necromantic energies are disrupted and the hazard is destroyed.
Gravehall Trap.routine:
(1 action) Undead fists assail all creatures in the hall, dealing @Damage[4d6[bludgeoning]] damage. Creatures in the hall must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:21].

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target takes half damage.

Failure The target takes full damage, and its Speeds are reduced by 10 feet (to a minimum of 5 feet) for 1 round.

Critical Failure The target takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} for 1 round.

Lunging Dead.name: Lunging Dead
Lunging Dead.description:
Trigger 生きているクリーチャーがトンネルの中央を横切ると発動する

Effect 壁や天井から死者が手を伸ばし、うめきながらうごめく蛆を吐き出す。部屋の生物は@Check[will|dc:21]のセーヴを試みる。罠はその後イニシアティブを振る.

Critical Success 対象は影響を受けない.

Success 対象は@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened}

となる

Failure 対象は@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}となる。罠の範囲内にいる間、恐怖状態を1未満には下げられない.

Critical Failure 失敗と同様だが、対象は@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed}となり、1 ラウンドの間続く.

Bottomless Pit.name: Bottomless Pit
Bottomless Pit.description: 鉄の落とし蓋が無限に深い10-フィートの正方形の穴を覆っている.
Bottomless Pit.stealth: (or 0 if the trapdoor is disabled or broken) or detect magic
Bottomless Pit.reset: The trap still causes creatures to fall forever if they fall in, but the trapdoor must be reset manually for the trap to become Hidden again.

Infinite Pitfall.name: Infinite Pitfall
Infinite Pitfall.description:
Trigger A creature walks onto the trapdoor

Effect The triggering creature falls in and continues to fall, potentially forever. That creature can try to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.3yoajuKjwHZ9ApUY]{Grab an Edge} to avoid falling. The DC to Climb the walls or Grab an Edge is 26. The pit contains many handholds, so the falling creature can try to Grab an Edge again every 6 seconds. If the creature succeeds, it can start to Climb out from that point (though it might be a very long climb, depending on how far the creature fell). Since the creature falls endlessly, it can rest and even prepare spells while falling, though items dropped while falling are usually lost forever.

Planar Rift.name: 次元の裂け目/Planar Rift
Planar Rift.description: A rift attempts to draw creatures into another plane (the GM chooses the specific plane).
Planar Rift.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:33|name:Assemble a Rift Seal|traits:action:disable-a-device] (master) to assemble a rift seal using objects strongly grounded to your plane, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG]{Dispel Magic} (7th rank; counteract DC 31) to counteract the rift

Into the Great Beyond.name: Into the Great Beyond
Into the Great Beyond.description:
Trigger 裂け目から10フィート以内に生物が移動する。

Effect 発生させた生物と裂け目から30フィート以内にいるすべての生物が別の次元へ引き込まれる。各生物はこの運命を避けるために@Check[reflex|dc:33]のセーヴを試みることができる.

Dance Of Death.name: Dance Of Death
Dance Of Death.description: An eerie orchestra compels all who hear it to dance until they collapse from exhaustion.
Dance Of Death.disable: @Check[intimidation|dc:42|name:Frighten the Orchestra|traits:action:demoralize] (expert) three times to frighten dancers and spectral musicians alike away from participating in the deadly performance, @Check[performance|dc:40|name:Disrupt Compulsion] (master) twice to produce a tune discordant enough to disrupt the compulsion, or @Check[religion|dc:42|name:Banish Spirits] (master) three times to banish the spirits with prayers
Dance Of Death.reset: The eerie orchestra spends an hour retuning its phantasmal instruments, after which it is ready to begin its routine again.
Dance Of Death.routine:
(1 action; auditory, incapacitation, mental, occult) The orchestra performs a raucous tune, compelling all creatures that can hear it to spend actions dancing. Each round, creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:37] save; the results of this save modify the number of actions that the creature must spend dancing each round. If this would cause the creature to spend more actions dancing than it can use on its turn, the creature takes @Damage[10d6] damage (or double that on a critical failure) from moving faster than its body can manage.

Critical Success The creature decreases the number of actions it must spend dancing by 1.

Success No effect.

Failure The creature increases the actions it must spend dancing by 1.

Critical Failure The creature increases the actions it must spend dancing by 2.

Prelude.name: Prelude
Prelude.description:
Trigger A creature approaches within 30 feet of the orchestra

Effect The orchestra compels all creatures that can hear it to begin dancing. Each creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:41] save, with the following effects. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} and cannot use reactions. Additionally, it must spend 1 of its actions each round dancing. Dancing is a move action that allows the creature to Stride up to half its Speed.

Failure As success, except the creature must spend 2 of its actions each round dancing.

Critical Failure As failure, except the creature must spend 3 of its actions each round dancing.
